recap: Argentina narrowly escaped Cape Verde's stunning upset bid with a 3-2 extra-time victory, with Cristian Romero's 111th-minute header deflecting off defender Diney Borges for the decisive own-goal after the underdogs twice equalized through Sidny Lopes Cabral and Deroy Duarte. Messi opened the scoring in the 29th minute with a clinical finish and added his record-extending 20th World Cup goal, while Lisandro Martínez restored Argentina's lead in the 103rd minute, but Cape Verde's 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha kept his minnow nation alive with 10 saves, including five against Messi. The reigning champions advanced to face Egypt in Atlanta on Tuesday, capping Cape Verde's remarkable World Cup debut in which the smallest nation ever to reach the knockout round had previously drawn with Spain, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia.
